mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 3.5% in a tightening cycle signals restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity valuations and reduce corporate borrowing capacity. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants price in slower growth and higher debt servicing costs.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of May 2008 indicates late-cycle tightening
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. This triggers equity market repricing downward as investors reduce growth expectations and increase equity risk premiums over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month combined with housing starts (HOUST) below 1,500k units signals weakening consumer demand and residential investment. This joint deterioration in demand indicators predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,425k as of April 2008 indicates housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both RSAFS declines and HOUST < 1500, or joint signal has lower predictive power than either signal alone (Rank IC difference > 0.10 in favor of single signals).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals contraction in manufacturing and capacity utilization, indicating supply-side weakness and potential earnings compression for cyclical equities. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.0% year-over-year while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4.0% signals stagflationary pressure. Central banks respond with sustained rate maintenance or further tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.35 and UNRATE at 3.7% as of May 2008 indicates tight labor market with inflation
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond CPIAUCSL alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown and recession risk. This triggers equity market repricing as investors reduce earnings growth expectations and increase risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% in latest quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration and rising recession probability. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings and growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300k units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) declining and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% signals a synchronized demand and monetary contraction. This triple constraint produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices recession probability upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1300 AND RSAFS month-over-month decline AND FEDFUNDS > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:All three conditions approaching or met as of June 2008
regime_hint: synchronized demand and monetary contraction with recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when all three conditions are met, or the three-way interaction has lower predictive power than any two-way subset (Rank IC difference > 0.08).
